data.title: Anthony Scaramucci's Journey from Bitcoin Skeptic to Advocate
data.description: According to PANews, Anthony Scaramucci, the founder and CEO of SkyBridge Capital, recently shared his transformation from a cryptocurrency skeptic to an advocate during a social media Q&A session. Scaramucci revealed that although he had the opportunity to engage with Bitcoin as early as 2012, it took him eight years to make his first Bitcoin investment. Initially, he did not understand Bitcoin and was highly skeptical of it. However, after gaining a deeper understanding of blockchain and Bitcoin, he recognized it as a significant technological breakthrough. Scaramucci further stated that with some research, 90% of people would likely lean towards Bitcoin.

data.title: Senator Chuck Schumer vows Democrats will force votes to reverse Trump’s tariffs
data.description:
Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer said Friday night that Democrats will “force votes” in the next few weeks to reverse President Donald Trump’s import tariffs, arguing the measures are hurting American workers. He pointed directly to the latest job report, which showed unemployment climbing and job growth slowing, and blamed Trump’s trade agenda for making […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/schumer-democrats-reverse-trumps-tariffs/

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Impact on Asset Classes Amid Debt Cycle Concerns
data.description: According to PANews, Joe Burnett, the strategic director at Semler Scientific, recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the current market situation. Burnett highlighted that the market is at the end of what Ray Dalio describes as the long-term debt cycle. This phase is characterized by extreme valuations in stocks, real estate, and fixed-income products. The culmination of the long-term debt cycle typically results in the devaluation of fiat currencies. Historically, gold has been considered the most reliable hard currency, but Burnett suggests that Bitcoin now holds that position. He posits that Bitcoin has the potential to significantly disrupt all asset classes.

data.title: The Wuxi Intermediate People's Court dismissed a case involving a "virtual dollar" investment dispute, leaving investors with little legal recourse for their losses.
data.description: The Wuxi Intermediate People's Court reported a case involving "virtual US dollars." A party entrusted another party to invest 84,350 yuan on a certain platform, exchanging it for 13,000 virtual US dollars. However, after the platform crashed, only 0.1 yuan could be withdrawn. The court found that the party made the investment independently and managed the account independently. However, the operation on the overseas platform was not properly registered, making it not protected by Chinese law. The court ultimately dismissed the lawsuit, ruling that the party bore the investment risk.

data.title: A large ETH holder transferred 3,711 to Binance, which could generate over $1.4 million in profit if sold.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai Yi @ai_9684 xtpa, a major investor who has been building an ETH position at an average price of $3,912 since two years ago deposited 3,711 ETH (approximately $15.93 million) to Binance 10 minutes ago. If sold, this would have realized a profit of approximately $1.417 million. Currently, this wallet still holds 3,000 ETH, with a paper profit of approximately $1.13 million.

data.title: Stripe CEO Patrick Collison Explains Why Businesses Are Turning to Stablecoins
data.description:
In a recent statement, Patrick Collison, the CEO of Stripe, elaborated on the growing trend among businesses to adopt stablecoins as a financial solution, discussing the various factors driving this shift and the potential benefits that stablecoins offer in terms of transaction efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and stability in a volatile market.

In a September 6 post on X, Martinez noted that if XRP faces another rejection at this level, the asset could retreat toward the $2
